original_hypothesis.title: Staking Limit Off-by-One Allows 11 NFTs Instead of 10
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The staking limit check uses `munchablesStaked[mainAccount].length > 10` instead of `>= 10`. When the array length is exactly 10 (indices 0-9 occupied), the check passes (10 > 10 = false), allowing one more push to reach 11 staked munchables. This exceeds the intended maximum of 10.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Code reads `if (munchablesStaked[mainAccount].length > 10) revert TooManyStakedMunchiesError()`. The check allows length=10 to pass, then push makes it 11. The intended behavior (suggested by error name and typical limit patterns) is max 10.

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (munchablesStaked[mainAccount].length > 10) should be >= 10 to enforce max of 10

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: length > 10 allows length=10 to pass; push makes 11
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: User stakes 11 NFTs instead of intended 10, gaining ~10% extra reward capacity
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: ~10% additional schnibbles farming capacity per account compared to intended maximum

economic_questions: Does the extra slot create meaningful economic advantage?
Could users create multiple accounts to multiply the effect?
Does the uint8 loop iterator in _farmPlots handle 11 elements safely? (Yes, 11 < 255)
validation_requirements: Confirm the off-by-one: stake 10, then stake 11th successfully

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Other farmers (slight dilution of relative rewards)

validation_result.impact_summary: The staking limit check allows 11 NFTs to be staked per account instead of the intended maximum of 10, granting ~10% extra reward farming capacity.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The `stakeMunchable` function checks `if (munchablesStaked[mainAccount].length > 10)` before pushing a new tokenId to the array. When the array length is exactly 10, the condition `10 > 10` evaluates to `false`, allowing the push to proceed and grow the array to 11 elements. The check only reverts when length is 11 (i.e., `11 > 10` is true), which means it's already too late — the 11th element was already added in a previous call. The intended maximum of 10 staked NFTs requires the check to use `>= 10` instead of `> 10`.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The defect is directly visible in the `stakeMunchable` function. The check `munchablesStaked[mainAccount].length > 10` followed by `munchablesStaked[mainAccount].push(tokenId)` allows the array to reach length 11. The error name `TooManyStakedMunchiesError` and the constant 10 clearly indicate the intended limit is 10. The `_farmPlots` function uses a `uint8` iterator which can handle 11 iterations (11 < 255), so the extra element does not cause an overflow there — it simply grants the user one extra staked NFT's worth of rewards.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The check uses `> 10` (strict greater-than) instead of `>= 10`. When the array has exactly 10 elements, the condition is false (10 > 10 == false), so execution continues and push() increases the length to 11. The correct check to enforce a maximum of 10 staked NFTs is `>= 10`.

calling_sequence: 1. User calls stakeMunchable(landlord, tokenId, plotId) with 10 NFTs already staked
6. Check: munchablesStaked[mainAccount].length > 10 — evaluates 10 > 10 = false — PASSES (should revert)
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The critical path is at step 6. When the user already has 10 staked munchables (array length 10), the condition `10 > 10` is false, so the revert does not trigger. The subsequent push at step 10 grows the array to 11 elements, exceeding the intended maximum of 10.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: The staking limit of 10 is hardcoded in the contract, not loaded from configuration. No deployment scripts affect this value.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: A user stakes 10 NFTs on various landlord plots (10 separate stakeMunchable calls). On the 11th call, the check `munchablesStaked[mainAccount].length > 10` evaluates to `10 > 10 = false` and does not revert. The 11th NFT is staked successfully, and the user now has 11 staked munchables. All 11 NFTs generate schnibbles during _farmPlots, giving the user ~10% more farming capacity than the intended 10-NFT maximum. The 12th attempt would revert because `11 > 10 = true`.
validation_result.impact_assessment: This is a confirmed off-by-one code defect. Each account can stake 11 NFTs instead of the intended 10, gaining approximately 10% extra schnibbles farming capacity. While the individual economic impact is moderate, the defect violates the intended game rules. The `_farmPlots` uint8 loop iterator handles 11 iterations safely (11 < 255). The impact is systematic — all users can exploit this, potentially causing slight inflation in schnibbles supply.
recommended_mitigations: Change the check from `munchablesStaked[mainAccount].length > 10` to `munchablesStaked[mainAccount].length >= 10` to enforce the intended maximum of 10 staked NFTs.
Consider extracting the magic number 10 into a named constant (e.g., `MAX_STAKED_MUNCHABLES`) for clarity and maintainability.
Add a unit test verifying that staking an 11th NFT reverts with TooManyStakedMunchiesError.
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy LandManager and configure dependencies. 2. Register a renter account and a landlord account with sufficient plots. 3. Mint 11 MunchNFTs to the renter and approve them to LandManager. 4. Call stakeMunchable 10 times — all should succeed. 5. Call stakeMunchable an 11th time — confirm it succeeds (bug). 6. Assert munchablesStaked[renter].length == 11. 7. Call farmPlots and verify all 11 NFTs accrue rewards. 8. After fix (>= 10), repeat and confirm the 11th stake reverts.
uncertainties: The intended maximum could theoretically be 11 rather than 10, though the error name 'TooManyStakedMunchiesError' and the typical pattern of limit checks strongly suggest 10 is the intended maximum.
additional_testing_needed: Confirm the intended staking limit with the development team or specification documents.
Verify that _farmPlots correctly processes all 11 staked tokens without any unexpected behavior.
Test gas costs with 11 staked NFTs to ensure no out-of-gas issues in _farmPlots.
